title: Olympic White 1974 Stratocaster [DEMO VIDEO]
model: Fender Stratocaster
description:
This is a 1974 Fender Stratocaster in its original Olympic White finish. This guitar is all original aside from a refret, added strap pin, and a replaced middle pickup, which is dated to 1975. The tremolo arm had plumbers tape added to the threading to allow it to fit properly in the bridge. The trem arm attached is not original, but it is completely functional and feels great. Original trem arm will be included, but I had replaced it because it was super short. It still retains its original 3 way switch and comes with its original hard shell case. The finish on this guitar shows some honest play wear on the body and the open chord position on the fretboard, giving it some extra mojo. It is exceptionally lightweight for any time period (let alone the 70s) weighing in at 7 lbs 4 oz. This guitar is a beast and definitely does the Hendrix grey bottom Strat sound as well as I’ve ever heard. Check the video linked to hear it in action!

Message me for further details or photos.

Thanks!

title: Fender Fender Stratocaster ‘62 AVRI (American Vintage Reissue) Fullerton Era 1982- 3 tone sunburst 1982 - Sunburst
model: Fender Stratocaster ‘62 AVRI (American Vintage Reissue) Fullerton Era 1982- 3 tone sunburst
description:
Fullerton Era Fender Stratocaster ‘62 AVRI (American Vintage Reissue) from early 1983 (1-12-83 neck date / 12-20-82 body date). The guitar is in near mint natural condition considering it is already more than 40 years old. Minor checking and very lite player marks. The guitar is all original except the three way switch was changed to a five position one. The case is also mint and the original vintage tweed. The tremolo bar and ashtray bridge cover, hang tags and manual are included in the case. 

Fullerton Era reissues were the first ever made in 1982-1984. They are considered by many players and collectors as the closest to the pre-CBS original Strats. They were made by hand, using the same methods, templates and even tools as the pre-CBS ones. They were produced for a short period of time, between 1982 and 1984, before Fender moved from Fullerton to Corona in 1985 (where also CNC machinery production was implemented and replaced hand crafting).

Since they were made only for two years, by hand and paying extraordinary attention to detail and original specs, a relatively small number were produced. So, there are not many of them out there and this is a mint example which has become already a piece of Fender history. According to many, this will be even more sought after in the future and become the next “pre-CBS” strats.

title: Fender Fender Stratocaster Old Growth Redwood
model: Fender Stratocaster Old Growth Redwood
description:
Fender Stratocaster Old Growth Redwood 2014 Natural
This Fender is part of my select collection, and once again, it’s a truly special guitar. Has anyone ever seen a Fender with a body made of one-piece century-old sequoia? What could something like that possibly sound like?

Well—here it is. Aesthetically, the guitar is beautifully designed: a natural wood finish with grain running parallel to the strings, an anodized pickguard that matches the overall look, and a channel-bound neck—yet another rare feature from Fender.

Beyond the looks—which are fascinating—the first time I picked up this guitar, the word that came to mind was resonance. Unplugged, I strummed the strings and immediately thought, “Wow… this thing really resonates!” That moment was the beginning of my deep dive into tonewoods, one-piece vs multi-piece bodies, poly vs nitro vs natural finishes, and so on. I kept wondering why it resonated so much. Was it because it was a one-piece body? Because it was sequoia? Because it was century-old sequoia? Or a combination of everything? I was hooked. Today, I believe it’s less about whether the body is one piece and more about the wood itself.

The next thing that surprised me was the weight—this guitar is feather-light. It weighs almost nothing, and it’s wonderful to play. Sequoia is a very soft wood; the good part is that the guitar is incredibly lightweight, and the “downside” is that even small bumps can leave dents.

The final surprise was the plugged-in tone. It doesn’t sound exactly like a traditional Strat. It has more body, and it’s sweeter. Which led me to my next question: does the wood really change the sound that much? My answer is yes. It’s not the pickups—let’s be honest, most Fender pickups sound fairly similar. But this guitar has a unique voice of its own. Personally, I love how it sounds. It’s simply one of a kind, and its tone is genuinely inspiring.

With all that said, I won’t be selling this guitar for just any price. There are millions of guitars out there, but this one truly stands out from the rest.


Condition: The guitar is used. The photos show a few small dings and scratches, but considering its manufacturing year (2014) and the softness of sequoia, it’s in very good condition.

My Philosophy: My collection is not guided by conventional measures such as vintage year or celebrity ownership, as I hold a different perspective. I appreciate each guitar as a work of art—its beauty, craftsmanship, its exclusivity (being limited, scarce, or hard to obtain), the character revealed in the wood, and the individuality of its form.

Regarding the Sale: This guitar is listed here more as a "gallery" to showcase my collection than a desperate sale. The listed price should be considered a reference price, intended to discourage casual or "light" purchases. I am open to offers only from a true enthusiast who understands and is specifically seeking this particular instrument. If you are just browsing for "a good guitar," this is not the instrument for you. If you are passionate about this specific model and know why you want it, please make an offer, and I will then consider whether or not to sell.

Specifications: You can find the official specs for this model on the official Fender website or specialized retailers. (Please note: The photos on those sites are stock images. The photos in this listing are of the actual, hand-selected guitar you will receive.)

description:
The Fender Stratocaster Highway One HSS (2009) is a versatile solid-body electric guitar, combining classic Strat tone with modern playability. Featuring an HSS pickup configuration (humbucker at the bridge and two single coils at the middle and neck), it delivers a wide range of sounds—from warm, vintage cleans to powerful rock leads.

Fullerton Era reissues were the first ever made. They are considered by many players and collectors as the closest to the pre-CBS original Strats. They were made by hand, using the same methods, templates and even tools as the pre-CBS ones. They were produced for a short period of time, between 1982 and 1984, before Fender moved from Fullerton to Corona in 1985 (where also CNC machinery production was implemented and replaced hand crafting).

Since they were made only for two years, by hand and paying extraordinary attention to detail, original specs and quality control, a relatively small number were produced. So, there are not many of them out there and even less in Europe. 

To sum up, this is an amazing guitar which has become already a piece of Fender history. According to many, this will be even more sought after in the future and become the next “pre-CBS” strats.

This is a rare beauty. 

Built to commemorate the 30th anniversary of the establishment of the Fender Custom Shop and displayed on the Fender stand at the NAMM show in California.

The photo of the pink sheet shows all the specs so I won't list them here, but suffice to say this instrument was built with the most premium materials at the Custom Shop, including a beutiful ash top, AA flamed maple neck and AAA rosewood fingerboard. 

One detail which is worth pointing out is the compound neck radius which tapers from 9.5in to 12in, making it a modern guitar with a vintage look.  

The flame neck is a piece of art - both visually and from a tactile perspective, courtesy of the stripped finish which took place at the Custom Shop. 

This is obviously a reliced guitar, so "condition" is a relative term, but as far as I am concerned the guitar has been looked after and cared for and I know it is in exactly the same condition I bought it, which was mint. 

Sometimes reliced guitars look either fake or "too much" (including some of the Custom Shop efforts), and maybe I am biased, but I genuinely think they have done a sterling job on this one. Every scrape, nick, wood part, metal and plastic component looks authentic.

The ash top is extremely light, but also very resonant. It is an absolute joy to hold.

The pickups were wired by hand at the Custom Shop.

The colour is an authentic 3 shade sunburst. I am a bit OCD when it comes to colour and how it blends, and once I was set on purchasing a sunburst, I looked for particular hues for a long time and discarded quite a few instruments, including a beautiful sunburst used for one of Guitarist Magazine's covers. I am not sure if it comes through in the photos, but the yellow and red in particular are stunning - not too bright but vibrant enough to make everything congruous. 

I will surely be sad to let this one go, but these days I no longer play single coil guitars much and I have a cheaper Custom Shop Strat which I use for gigging. This one is just sitting in its case. It really deserves to be played. 

You can see the guitar being used at this festival 

I strongly encourage interested (but serious buyers) to come view the guitar with no obligation to purchase. Photos don't do it justice and this instrument needs to be seen (and felt) in person. I have a Fender Tone Master amp, which can be used for a sound test. 


Any other questions - please ask.

description:
Produit d'occasion en dépôt-vente / Garantie 3 mois / Contactez le magasin de Bordeaux pour plus d’informations.

Fender Custom Shop 50’s Stratocaster Relic “White Blonde” – Période Vince Cunetto

Véritable pièce de collection issue de la très recherchée période Vince Cunetto, cette Stratocaster Relic représente l’une des productions les plus emblématiques du Fender Custom Shop des années 90. Une époque aujourd’hui considérée par de nombreux passionnés comme l’âge d’or du relic artisanal chez Fender.

Produite durant l’ère Cunetto, cette guitare bénéficie d’un travail de vieillissement réalisé avec une approche encore très artisanale, bien avant l’industrialisation des procédés relic modernes. Vince Cunetto, artisan indépendant mandaté par le Fender Custom Shop au milieu des années 90, est devenu une figure culte pour la qualité exceptionnelle de ses finitions vieillies, reconnues pour leur réalisme, leur finesse et leur caractère unique. Les instruments issus de cette période sont aujourd’hui particulièrement recherchés pour leur authenticité visuelle, leur résonance et leur sensation immédiatement “jouée”.

Cette 50’s Stratocaster Relic en finition White Blonde repose sur un superbe corps en frêne deux pièces associé à un manche érable flammé au profil Soft “V”, offrant une prise en main typiquement vintage : confortable, vivante et extrêmement inspirante. Le vernis nitrocellulosique relic laisse pleinement respirer le bois et participe à cette sensation acoustique ouverte et réactive qui fait la réputation des meilleures productions Custom Shop.

Côté sonorités, le trio de micros Custom Shop simple bobinage délivre tout ce que l’on attend d’une grande Strat des années 50 : graves boisés, médiums organiques, aigus précis et dynamiques, avec ce claquant typique immédiatement identifiable. Les positions intermédiaires offrent un registre creusé particulièrement musical, idéal pour les sons funky, blues, rock vintage ou clean cristallin. Une guitare expressive, réactive au toucher et incroyablement nuancée selon l’attaque.

L’accastillage doré apporte une élégance rare à l’ensemble tout en sublimant la finition White Blonde subtilement vieillie. Le vibrato vintage complète parfaitement l’esprit de l’instrument avec une réponse souple et musicale.

Une Stratocaster d’exception, aussi inspirante à jouer qu’intéressante pour un collectionneur averti, représentant une période devenue mythique dans l’histoire du Fender Custom Shop.

Blonde Burst.  Alder body finished in see through blonde. Medium profile maple neck with a 9.5” radius rosewood  fingerboard and dot inlays. Locking tuners and rolling nut. The nut width is 42.5mm. The bridge is a Wilkinson. Comes with the factory original Floyd Rose licensed bridge (The G string saddle had it's underneath locking screw snapped during a setup - as these are rare, it has so far been impossible to replace just the G saddle. The pickups are all original Fender Lace sensor. Standard wiring and 5 way toggle switch. Great playing and sounding strat. Has a few body finish marks as seen in photos, 2 small ones by the base and one bigger one on the body top by the strap holder. Not visible head on. Two screw holes below bridge pickup that used to hold a GR 300 hex pickup. One owner, me, used for a lot of gigs in 90s and early oughts. In good overall condition,

title: Fender Stratocaster Player II Black
model: Fender Stratocaster Player II Black
description:
La Fender Player II Series è stata introdotta nel 2023 come seconda generazione della serie “Player” che a sua volta nel 2018 ha sostituito le mitiche Fender “Made in Mexico”.

Questa nuova linea propone strumenti che a dispetto di un prezzo level-entry presentano standard qualitativi e finiture analoghe ai modelli delle serie più blasonate.

Disponibili i modelli Stratocaster, Telecaster, Jazz e  Precision Bass in numerose colorazioni fra le quali i tradizionali 3-Color Sunburst e Polar White oltre alle nuovissime finiture Aquatone Blue, Coral Red, Birch Green, Hialeah Yellow e Black. La possibilità di finiture satinate e l’opzione di tastiere in acero o palissandro completano la dotazione complessiva della linea che ora dispone di oltre 80 modelli fra chitarre e bassi.

Le novità rispetto alle serie precedenti includono miglioramenti dell’hardware e, in generale, della qualità complessiva di realizzazione.

Gli strumenti presentano body in ontano e manici con profilo “modern C”, ancora più ergonomici e con radius leggermente piatto (9.5”); le tastiere hanno bordi smussati per un miglior confort ed una migliore suonabilità.

I pickup hanno magneti in alnico V e sono stati ridisegnati per migliorare la dinamica e la risposta timbrica. Ridisegnati anche i ponti, maggiormente curati nella realizzazione e nel design delle sellette. Concludono la dotazione le meccaniche ClassicGear (già adottate sulle serie superiori), più precise e stabili nella tenuta dell’accordatura.

Gli strumenti della serie Fender Player II Series rappresentano la migliore scelta per coloro che cercano una chitarra o un basso che garantiscano un salto di qualità rispetto ai modelli entry-level, il tutto ad un prezzo molto contenuto rispetto ai modelli di fascia più alta.

title: Fender Stratocaster SSS American Ultra II Noble Blue
model: Fender Stratocaster SSS American Ultra II Noble Blue
description:
Chitarra elettrica di fascia alta che unisce il tradizionale design Stratocaster a una serie di innovazioni tecniche pensate per offrire al chitarrista professionista una suonabilità eccezionale e grande versatilità.

Il corpo è realizzato in ontano selezionato, leggero e bilanciato, con finitura “Noble Blue”,  un blu profondo e raffinato, lucido e intenso, che conferisce allo strumento un look elegante e accattivante, valorizzando le forme classiche della Stratocaster. Il design ergonomico è stato ottimizzato nella lavorazione della tastiera e nella congiunzione del manico alla cassa per garantire comfort anche nelle lunghe sessioni di studio o live.

Il manico è in acero roasted quartersawn, quindi trattato termicamente per una migliore stabilità e una risposta tonale più precisa. Il profilo è un “Modern D”, apprezzato per la sua comodità e adattabilità a diversi stili di esecuzione. La tastiera è in ebano con radius variabile 10-14” e 22 tasti medium-jumbo facilitando l’esecuzione veloce e fluida di bending e fraseggi complessi.

L’elettronica è composta da tre pickup “Ultra Noiseless Vintage single coil” che offrono il classico suono brillante, aperto e dinamico della Stratocaster, ma senza il rumore di fondo tipico dei single coil tradizionali. Questi pickup permettono di spaziare da toni cristallini e puliti a suoni più spinti e saturi, rendendo la chitarra adatta a una vasta gamma di generi musicali. L’elettronica include lo switch brevettato S-1™ che attiva in modo intelligente configurazioni aggiuntive dei pickup per una varietà timbrica ancor più ampia.

Il ponte è un tremolo sincronizzato American Ultra con ancoraggio a due pivot, leva pop-in (innesto a baionetta), sellette in acciaio inossidabile lucidato e blocco in acciaio laminato a freddo.

Le meccaniche sono le Deluxe autobloccanti per una tenuta dell’accordatura perfetta.

La Fender Stratocaster SSS American Ultra II Noble Blue è uno strumento pensato per il chitarrista esigente che desidera un autentico suono Stratocaster con tutte le innovazioni moderne per prestazioni elevate, suonabilità eccezionale e un’estetica elegante e contemporanea.
